items.title: Presented MoM Framework at Online Meetup
items.content: Presented the MoM (Mixture of Models) framework in an online meetup, followed by engaging discussions with the audience on LLM routing and model selection strategies.

items.title: Teaching Assistant at Royal Holloway
items.content: Started as a Teaching Assistant for Object-Oriented Programming & Software Engineering at Royal Holloway, University of London.

items.title: Product Data Intern at [Stats Perform](https://www.statsperform.com/)
items.content: Joined Stats Perform as a Product Data Intern, working on sports analytics and data science.

items.title: ML Research Assistant at Royal Holloway
items.content: Started as an ML Research Assistant at Royal Holloway, working on transformer attention mechanisms and adversarial robustness.

items.title: Founded [Nordlys Labs](https://www.nordlyslabs.com/)
items.content: Co-founded Nordlys Labs, building intelligent LLM routing systems that select the best model for each task. Beat frontier models on SWE-bench benchmarks. Scaled to 500+ users.

items.title: Started BSc CS & AI at [Royal Holloway](https://www.royalholloway.ac.uk/)
items.content: Began my Bachelor's degree in Computer Science & Artificial Intelligence at Royal Holloway, University of London.

items.title: Nordlys Labs
items.description: Intelligent LLM routing startup that selects the best model for each task. Beat frontier models on SWE-bench benchmarks. Scaled to 500+ users.
items.longDescription:
## About

Nordlys Labs is an intelligent LLM routing startup that selects the best model for each task, reducing AI inference costs by 50-60%. We were selected by the Antler accelerator and scaled to over 500 users.

## Key Achievements

- Beat frontier models on SWE-bench benchmarks
- Reduced AI inference costs by 50-60%
- Scaled to 500+ active users
- Selected by the Antler accelerator program

items.title: KV-cache optimization
items.description: Built a transformer architecture from scratch, implementing attention mechanisms, positional encoding, and training pipelines from first principles.
items.longDescription:
## About

A deep dive into transformer architectures, building every component from scratch to develop a thorough understanding of how large language models work.

## What I Built

- Multi-head self-attention mechanism
- Positional encoding (sinusoidal and learned)
- Training pipelines with gradient accumulation
- Tokenizer integration and text generation

items.title: Readyou
items.description: Dynamic README generator that automatically creates beautiful and informative repository documentation from your codebase.
items.longDescription:
## About

Readyou is a dynamic README generator that analyzes your codebase and automatically creates beautiful, informative repository documentation.

## Features

- Automatic codebase analysis
- Beautiful markdown generation
- Support for multiple programming languages
- Customizable templates and themes

items.title: Transformer From Stones
items.description: Implementing a transformer model from first principles, building each component from the ground up to deeply understand the architecture.
items.longDescription:
## About

A from-scratch implementation of the transformer architecture, building each component from the ground up to develop deep understanding of the model.

## Implementation Details

- Layer normalization and residual connections
- Scaled dot-product attention
- Feed-forward networks with GELU activation
- Byte-pair encoding tokenization

items.title: Cyber Attack Detection
items.description: Machine learning-based threat detection system that identifies and classifies cyber attacks using network traffic analysis.
items.longDescription:
## About

A machine learning-based threat detection system that identifies and classifies cyber attacks by analyzing network traffic patterns.

## Approach

- Feature engineering on network traffic data
- Multiple ML classifiers (Random Forest, SVM, Neural Networks)
- Real-time classification pipeline
- High accuracy on benchmark datasets

items.title: MoM Framework
items.description: Research paper on the Mixture of Models (MoM) framework for intelligent LLM routing and model selection strategies.
items.longDescription:
## About

The MoM (Mixture of Models) framework is a research contribution to intelligent LLM routing. It introduces a systematic approach to selecting the best model for each task, combining routing strategies with cost optimization.

## Key Contributions

- Novel framework for LLM model selection
- Cost-aware routing strategies
- Benchmarking across multiple model families
- Presented at an online meetup with community engagement

items.title: Git-RLMs
items.description: Recursive Language Models for git repository analysis, enabling LLM-based code exploration through incremental data filtering.
items.longDescription:
## About

Git-RLM applies Recursive Language Models to analyze git repositories. Based on research showing that long prompts should be stored as environment variables for programmatic interaction, it enables models to explore repos incrementally.

## Features

- CLI and Python API for querying repositories
- Examine commit histories and file contents at specific versions
- Search code, analyze authorship, and compare changes
- Sandboxed Python environment for safe execution
- Support for multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Anthropic)
- Trajectory logging and cost management

items.title: Vantage
items.description: AI ROI Calculator that evaluates AI implementation feasibility and returns strategic investment assessments in minutes.
items.longDescription:
## About

Vantage is a strategic AI investment assessment platform that compresses 40+ consultant hours into a 5-10 minute evaluation. It combines financial modeling, industry benchmarking, and AI analysis.

## Key Capabilities

- Feasibility scoring across technical complexity, data readiness, and risk
- Three-year financial projections with risk-adjusted returns
- Industry benchmarking grounded in 200+ real-world case studies
- Build-vs-buy analysis and implementation roadmaps

## Tech Stack

Uses Claude API with LangGraph for multi-step reasoning, Sentence Transformers for semantic search, and ChromaDB for vector-based case study retrieval.

items.title: Get Me Clients
items.description: AI-powered lead generation tool for SMEs, targeting the pharmaceutical industry with location-based client discovery across Algeria.
items.longDescription:
## About

A lead generation platform designed for small and medium enterprises. The tool helps sales teams identify potential clients using location-based data and AI-powered analysis.

## Features

- Location-based lead discovery across 58 regions
- Google Maps API integration for geographical queries
- AI-powered lead analysis with DeepSeek
- FastAPI backend with Streamlit frontend

items.title: Sales Assistant
items.description: AI-powered sales assistant application built with Next.js to help sales teams streamline their workflow.
items.longDescription:
## About

An AI-powered sales assistant designed to help sales teams be more productive. Built as a full-stack web application with a modern UI.

## Features

- AI-driven sales insights and recommendations
- Modern, responsive web interface
- Real-time interaction capabilities

items.title: Claude with Ads
items.description: Ad-supported AI chat application providing free access to Claude-level intelligence, funded by contextual ads from Thrad's network.
items.longDescription:
## About

An ad-supported AI chat application that gives users free access to Claude-level AI, funded by contextual advertisements. After each AI response, contextual ads from Thrad's ad network are displayed.

## Features

- Real-time message streaming
- Email-based authentication via verification codes
- Contextual ad integration with Thrad network
- Redis-based data persistence
- Full-stack deployment on Vercel

items.title: Altair
items.description: A React and TypeScript web application built with Vite for fast development and optimized builds.
items.longDescription:
## About

Altair is a web application built with React, TypeScript, and Vite, leveraging modern frontend tooling for fast development and optimized production builds.

items.title: Prompt Repetition Experiment
items.description: Research replication testing whether prompt repetition improves small language models' accuracy, achieving near-doubling on ARC-Challenge benchmarks.
items.longDescription:
## About

A research experiment investigating whether repeating prompts within a single request boosts accuracy on standardized benchmarks for small language models.

## Key Findings

- Tested 9 open-source models (0.5B to 9B parameters)
- Benchmarks: ARC-Challenge, OpenBookQA, GSM8K, HellaSwag, NameIndex
- ARC-Challenge accuracy nearly doubled from 22% to 44% with triple repetition (p=0.022)
- Leverages transformer left-to-right processing for quasi-bidirectional understanding

items.title: LSTM from Scratch
items.description: Implementation of Long Short-Term Memory neural networks from first principles, building the architecture from the ground up.
items.longDescription:
## About

A from-scratch implementation of LSTM (Long Short-Term Memory) networks, building every gate and cell state mechanism from first principles to understand recurrent neural network architectures.

## Implementation

- Forget, input, and output gates
- Cell state management
- Backpropagation through time
- Training on sequence data

items.title: Mini Compiler
items.description: A mini compiler built with Lex for lexical analysis and parsing, exploring compiler design and language processing.
items.longDescription:
## About

A mini compiler implementation covering lexical analysis and parsing. Built as a deep dive into compiler design principles and language processing fundamentals.

## Components

- Lexical analyzer using Lex
- Token generation and parsing
- Syntax analysis

items.title: Harry Potter Text Generator
items.description: Character-level RNN text generator trained on Harry Potter books, exploring sequence modeling, vanishing gradients, and the limits of recurrent architectures.
items.longDescription:
## About

A character-level text generation project using Recurrent Neural Networks trained on the Harry Potter corpus. The goal was to understand sequence modeling from first principles and observe where vanilla RNNs break down.

## Technical Approach

- Character-level tokenization with one-hot encoding
- Vanilla RNN cells with tanh activation and hidden state propagation
- Cross-entropy loss with temperature-controlled softmax sampling
- Backpropagation through time (BPTT) with gradient clipping

## Lessons Learned

- Vanishing gradients make it difficult for vanilla RNNs to capture long-range dependencies, the model loses coherence beyond short phrases
- Without gating mechanisms (LSTM/GRU), the hidden state saturates and forgets earlier context
- Temperature tuning is critical: too low produces repetitive text, too high produces gibberish
- This project motivated my subsequent LSTM from Scratch implementation to address these limitations
